title: TIG Welding Setup: Cable and Shielding Gas Connection
summary: This section covers the step-by-step setup procedure for TIG welding mode on the Vulcan OmniPro 220, including connecting the ground clamp, TIG torch, foot pedal, and shielding gas. It also addresses connecting the power cord. Both sold-separately and included components are identified.
key_facts: Ground Clamp Cable plugs into the Positive Socket and locks clockwise.
TIG Torch Cable plugs into the Negative Socket and locks clockwise.
The TIG Torch is sold separately.
The Foot Pedal is sold separately.
Foot Pedal Cable inserts through a hole on the Welder front and connects inside.
The Foot Pedal Socket is located inside the Welder.
The Foot Pedal Cable collar is secured by turning clockwise until tight.
Shielding Gas requires 100% Argon cylinder, not included.
The Argon cylinder must be secured with two straps to prevent tipping.
The Regulator is included with the Welder.
The cylinder valve should be briefly opened to blow dust before connecting the regulator.
The power cord plugs into either a 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power Input Socket.
procedure_steps: 1. Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Positive Socket. Twist clockwise all the way to lock in place.
2. Plug TIG Torch Cable (TIG Torch sold separately) into Negative Socket. Twist clockwise all the way to lock in place.
3. Insert the Foot Pedal Cable (Foot Pedal sold separately) through hole on Welder front and connect to the Foot Pedal Socket inside the machine. Secure by turning collar clockwise until tight.
4. With assistance, place a 100% Argon cylinder (not included) onto a cabinet or cart near the Welder and secure the cylinder in place with two straps (not included) to prevent tipping.
5. Remove the cylinder's cap. Stand to the side of the valve opening, then open the valve briefly to blow dust and dirt from the valve opening. Close the cylinder valve.
6. Locate the Regulator (included) and close its valve until it is loose, then thread Regulator onto cylinder and wrench-tighten connection.
7. Connect Shielding Gas Hose on TIG Torch Cable Connector to the Regulator's Outlet and wrench-tighten connection.
8. Plug either 120 VAC or 240 VAC Power Cord into Power Input Socket. Note: Plug will only fit one way.
warnings: TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY FROM ACCIDENTAL OPERATION: Turn the Power Switch off and unplug the Welder before setup.
WARNING! TO PREVENT SERIOUS INJURY FROM ACCIDENTAL OPERATION: Do not plug cord into wall outlet at this time.
Read the ENTIRE IMPORTANT SAFETY INFORMATION section at the beginning of this manual including all text under subheadings therein before set up or use of this product.
